system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

The content resolution is correct — `"home.services"` splits to `['home', 'services']` and resolves to `d['home']['services']`. That object exists with 8 items. So why does the services section render empty?

The issue is line 222 in compose-site.ts: `const resolved = s.content ? resolveRef(s.content, copyCtx) : {}`. This resolves `"home.services"` and gets the full services object. Then this `resolved` object is passed as `props` to the component. The ServicesSection component receives `{...resolved}` as props, which means it should get `title`, `subtitle`, `items`, `categories`, etc.

BUT — the section builder ALSO fires first at line 168. The builder for 'services' looks at `business` data (not content), finds no relevant data, returns null. Then `normalized` stays null, and the code falls through to content-ref. The content-ref uses `resolveRef('home.services', copyCtx)` which should return the full services data.

Wait — I bet the issue is that `resolveRef` returns the services OBJECT, but the component props expect the individual fields to be spread. The props object at line 222-225:
```ts
const props = {
  ...fillDeep(resolved as Record<string, unknown>, placeholders),
  ...__commonContext,
}
```
This spreads `resolved` (which is `{title, subtitle, items, ...}`) into props. The component receives `title`, `subtitle`, `items`. That should work.

  beforeAfter: buildBeforeAfter,", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

There it is! `buildServices` returns `services: business.services` (from business data) or `resolveServicesFromContent(servicesContent)`. But the content is passed as `{}` (empty), so `(content as ServicesContent).servicesPage` is `undefined`, and `business.services` is also undefined (no Supabase data for dayah). So the builder returns `{title: 'Nuestros Servicios', services: undefined, ...}`.

The `headline || title` check at line 168 of compose-site.ts — `title` is 'Nuestros Servicios' (non-empty), so `normalized` gets set to the builder result (with `services: undefined`). Then the content-ref is NEVER reached, because `normalized` is already set.

The fix: the builder returns `services: undefined` but passes the `headline || title` check because of the hardcoded title. Then the component gets `services: undefined` → `services.length` throws because `undefined` has no `.length`.

But wait — the component does `const services = servicesProp || items || []` at line 101. If `servicesProp` is `undefined`, it falls to `items` which is also `undefined` (the builder result only has `services`, not `items`). So `services` becomes `[]`. The component returns the "Servicios próximamente" fallback.

The fix: make the builder return null when `resolveServicesFromContent` returns empty:

Line 218-219: `Object.entries(compVal as Record<string, string>)` — the `cols-lg` value is `4` (number), not a string. When cast to `Record<string, string>`, the number silently becomes a string via the `as string` cast but wait... `propVal` is typed as `string` inside `Record<string, string>`. Since `cols-lg` is `4` (a number), the `Object.entries` still works but `propVal as string` coerces it to `"4"`. So `--portfolio-cols-lg: 4` in CSS. That's correct — `grid-cols-[var(--portfolio-cols-lg,3)]` reads `4` as a number of columns. But CSS custom properties are always strings, so it becomes `grid-template-columns: 4` which is invalid — it needs `repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr))`.

The CSS variable needs to store `repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr))` or I need to change the component:

Then compose-site falls to content-ref, which does `resolveRef('home.services', copyCtx)`. That returns the full object `{title, subtitle, categories, items, ...}`. Then `mergeOverrides` and `fillDeep` process it. Then `normalizeSectionProps` is called — which does `if (normalized.items && !normalized.services) { normalized.services = normalized.items }`.

{"output": "# =============================================================================
# Paragu-AI Builder — Production Dockerfile
# Multi-stage build for optimized Next.js deployment on VPS (Hostinger)
#
# Build context must be the REPO ROOT (so src/ and sites/ are available):
#   docker build -f web/Dockerfile \
#     --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=... \
#     --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=... \
#     --build-arg NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=... \
#     -t paragu-ai:prod .
#
# Layout inside the runner:
#   /app/server.js          Next.js standalone entrypoint
#   /app/.next/static/      static assets (JS/CSS bundles)
#   /app/public/            static public files
#   /app/src/               design tokens, schemas, registry, verticals
#   /app/sites/             per-tenant JSON (site.json, pages, content)
# =============================================================================

# Stage 1: Dependencies
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat python3 make g++
COPY web/package.json web/package-lock.json* ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
    npm install --legacy-peer-deps

# Stage 2: Builder
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app

# Public build args — safe to embed (exposed in browser)
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL

# Private secrets passed via BuildKit --secret (not embedded in image)
#   docker build --secret id=supabase_key,env=SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY ...
#   Run with: echo $SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL:-[REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]}
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=${NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY:-eyJhbG...SXRg}
ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=${NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL:-https://paragu-ai.com}
# Secrets are made available at build time via BuildKit --secret mounts
# and consumed in the build stage below.

# Copy source tree (repo root is build context)
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./web/node_modules
COPY web ./web
COPY src ./src
COPY sites ./sites
# CHANGELOG.md is read at build time by web/app/changelog/page.tsx (via
# process.cwd() + '..'). Without this file the static-generation step
# throws ENOENT and the build fails. Keep this copy until the changelog
# page is refactored to embed content at generate-tenant-data time.
COPY CHANGELOG.md ./CHANGELOG.md

# Build inside web/
WORKDIR /app/web
COPY web/.cachebust ./.cachebust
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV SRC_DIR=/app/src
ENV SITES_DIR=/app/sites
RUN --mount=type=secret,id=supabase_key,env=SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY \
    --mount=type=secret,id=google_places_key,env=GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY \
    npx next build && \
    for d in /app/web/.next/server/app/s/*/; do \
      base=$(basename "$$d"); \
      [ "$$base" != "[locale]" ] && rm -rf "$$d"; \
    done && \
    for d in /app/web/.next/standalone/.next/server/app/s/*/; do \
      [ -d "$$d" ] && base=$(basename "$$d"); \
      [ "$$base" != "[locale]" ] && rm -rf "$$d"; \
    done && \
    echo "Cleaned locale shadow dirs"

# Stage 3: Runner (Production)
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
ENV SRC_DIR=/app/src
ENV SITES_DIR=/app/sites

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

# Standalone output contains server.js + minimal node_modules.
# Copy alongside: public/, .next/static/, src/, sites/ so the runtime
# can serve static assets + read tenant + token JSON.
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/web/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/web/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/web/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/src ./src
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/sites ./sites

# Next.js 16 creates static locale dirs that shadow [locale] — remove them
RUN for d in /app/.next/server/app/s/*/; do \
      base=$(basename "$$d"); \
      [ "$$base" != "[locale]" ] && rm -rf "$$d"; \
    done && echo "Cleaned locale shadow dirs"

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=60s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://127.0.0.1:3000/api/health || exit 1

# Cleanup Next.js 16 locale shadow dirs that break [locale] dynamic routing
COPY --chown=nextjs:nodejs web/docker-start.sh /app/docker-start.sh
RUN chmod +x /app/docker-start.sh

CMD ["/app/docker-start.sh"]", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

1. **Services section builder pattern**: PAB's `buildServices` returns a non-null object with hardcoded `title` even when no service items exist, which BLOCKS the content-ref fallback. Fix: return `null` when `resolveServicesFromContent` returns empty. This is a generic PAB engine fix, not a dayah-specific fix — worth adding to the `paragu-ai-builder` skill as a known pitfall.

2. **Portfolio grid CSS variable pattern**: The grid columns are hardcoded `lg:grid-cols-3` — made configurable via `--portfolio-cols-lg` CSS var, matching the gap pattern. Same approach works for any Tailwind grid column count.

3. **WhatsApp float phone resolution**: Falls back through `footer.whatsapp` → `contact.whatsapp` → needs `footer.whatsappLink` as third fallback for sites that only store the URL.

These three patterns are PAB engine maintenance, not a new skill domain. I'll update the existing `paragu-ai-builder` skill to document these fixes when context allows — but the session ran out of tool calls before I could do it. The deploy lesson is saved to memory.
